Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Shaun - 
There are several tests that hang in the latest round of testing for this patch. The sanity-lnet test 103 timeout for this patch, at https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/36c6122e-53f2-4db2-9181-7114aa4c6081, is the only timeout for this test in the past six months. Is there any way this patch could be causing sanity-lnet test 103 and other tests to hang?

Patch Set 2:

James,

This is pretty far outside of LNet code paths. I am sure there is nothing in this patch that can be having an effect on sanity-lnet 103, the test is adding and removing tcp (gateway) routes. Nothing related to DIO or O_APPEND.

Looking at the console output test 103 hung on reinit_dlc.

According to the spew in the log there is a deadlock at peer.c:2135:

	/*
	 * Slow path:
	 * use the lnet_api_mutex to serialize the creation of the peer_ni
	 * and the creation/deletion of the local ni/net. When a local ni is
	 * created, if there exists a set of peer_nis on that network,
	 * they need to be traversed and updated. When a local NI is
	 * deleted, which could result in a network being deleted, then
	 * all peer nis on that network need to be removed as well.
	 *
	 * Creation through traffic should also be serialized with
	 * creation through DLC.
	 */
	lnet_net_unlock(cpt);
	mutex_lock(&the_lnet.ln_api_mutex);  <--- DEAD LOCK HERE
---
There are 2 tasks trying to take the same lock:

This suggests there may be some sort of kernel bug?

5.4.0-96-generic was released 18-Jan-2022 based on v5.4.157 there were a couple of
sched related landed later, possibily
 - sched/core: Mitigate race cpus_share_cache()/update_top_cache_domain() (Vincent Donnefort)
which is included in 5.4.0-97-generic on the 19th ...

> Shaun -
 > There are several tests that hang in the latest round of testing

 > https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/36c6122e-53f2-4db2-9181-7114aa4c6081,
 > is the only timeout for this test in the past six months. Is there
 > any way this patch could be causing sanity-lnet test 103 and other
 > tests to hang?

James, Shaun, I think the sanity-lnet failure is a red herring.  I  think this patch is very unlikely to cause hangs in sanity-lnet, but _could_ be causing some of the timeouts in the Lustre/IO related tests (assuming they are using O_DIRECT).  I've retriggered the test failures to see if they persist, or if they are just intermittent failures.

commit.subject: LU-15670 clio: Disable lockless for DIO with O_APPEND
commit.message:
LU-15670 clio: Disable lockless for DIO with O_APPEND

Lockless O_DIRECT with O_APPEND can allow interleaved / racy
appends from concurrent I/O.

Disable lockless I/O when O_APPEND is set

HPE-bug-id: LUS-9776
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
